investment_grade.summary: The halftime under thesis was logical from efficiency regression, but the model underweighted OKC's low-turnover control and SGA's sustained ceiling.

actual_vs_model_delta.miss_reason: Second half scoring did not regress enough. OKC maintained elite offensive efficiency through SGA and bench units, and PHX contributed enough shot volume/FT efficiency to push total past 223.5.

Shai Gilgeous-Alexander.note: Primary model miss. SGA's perfect-ish efficiency sustained beyond expected halftime regression.

Alex Caruso.note: Bench unit stabilization and spacing contributor.

Dillon Brooks.note: Sustained PHX scoring and helped over survive.

Devin Booker.note: Below primary scoring expectation, but PHX still reached 109 through Brooks/Green.

Jalen Green.note: Secondary scoring kept team total alive.

Collin Gillespie.note: Unexpected support through boards and facilitation.

okc_best_live_unit.note: Bench/small unit created separation and undercut under thesis.

phx_starting_lineup.note: PHX starting lineup was offensively good enough, but could not defend OKC.

hit_miss_reason.primary: The under lost because OKC's offensive process was not merely hot shooting; it was low-turnover, SGA-driven rim/FT efficiency plus productive bench spacing. PHX also did not collapse offensively, reaching 109 with Brooks and Green carrying scoring.
hit_miss_reason.market_error: The 223.5 line at halftime did not sufficiently account for SGA ceiling and PHX contribution through FT efficiency.
hit_miss_reason.model_error: Regression gate was too aggressive and did not identify OKC low-turnover control as sustainable enough.

model_patch_recommendations.description: Do not aggressively fade first-half efficiency when a high-usage superstar has rim/FT control, low turnovers, and clean support spacing.
model_patch_recommendations.trigger: Primary star TS% extreme with team TOV% below 10 and lead/control maintained.

model_patch_recommendations.description: Upgrade second-half total projection when one team has elite ball security and no pace crash.
model_patch_recommendations.trigger: Team TOV% <= 9%, pace >= 96, and primary offense remains on-court 36+ minutes.

model_patch_recommendations.description: Raise total floor when bench/small units create high ORTG spacing bursts without sacrificing pace.
model_patch_recommendations.trigger: Non-starting unit posts 145+ ORTG for 7+ minutes with positive plus-minus.

model_patch_recommendations.description: Do not downgrade PHX team total too hard when Booker is muted but Brooks/Green are carrying scoring efficiently.
model_patch_recommendations.trigger: Two secondary scorers combine for 45+ projected points by halftime/final trend.

final_verdict.summary: OKC@PHX Game 3 was a live total model miss. The halftime no-over position underestimated OKC's sustainable offensive control through SGA and low turnovers. The game finished 230, clearing the 223.5 line by 6.5 points. This creates a key patch: do not treat all first-half efficiency spikes as regression candidates when superstar control, low turnovers, and spacing units are intact.

source_note: Boxscore and team stats supplied by user; lineup stats supplied directly in chat.
